commands.name: schema
commands.description: Print CLI schema.

commands.name: ask
commands.description: Interpret a small natural-language request such as checking, releasing, listing history, or rolling back a configured service, then execute the matching service-level OpsPilot command. Use --dry-run to only show the interpreted command.

commands.name: capabilities
commands.description: Return available and missing troubleshooting evidence sources, including Kubernetes, Pod logs, Prometheus, ELK/OpenSearch, APISIX logs, Docker node-agent, GitLab/GitOps/Registry, and Argo CD.

commands.name: doctor
commands.description: Check CLI/backend reachability and summarize available or missing evidence sources. Use this before deeper AI-assisted troubleshooting.

commands.name: skills registry
commands.description: Return the OpsPilot skills registry that maps integrated Codex skills to deterministic OpsPilot commands, evidence sources, boundaries, and AI follow-up routing.

commands.name: skills validate
commands.description: Validate the server-side GitLab skills repository, or a local skills directory when --dir is provided for development checks.

commands.name: skills sources
commands.description: List mirrored upstream skill sources, registry path, candidate counts, and unsupported counts from the server-side skills mirror.

commands.name: skills candidates
commands.description: List mirrored candidate skills and unsupported skills that are not yet enabled as OpsPilot runtime skills.

commands.name: skills discover
commands.description: Automatically discover mirrored skill candidates and score whether they fit OpsPilot server-side runtime boundaries. Does not write files or enable skills.

commands.name: skills review
commands.description: Review one mirrored candidate, return suitability score, blockers, missing mappings, and next steps before import-plan or GitLab promotion.

commands.name: skills import-plan
commands.description: Generate a dry-run, reviewable runtime skill draft for one mirrored candidate. Does not write files or enable the skill.

commands.name: skills promote
commands.description: Dry-run alias for skills import-plan. Real promotion must happen through reviewed GitLab skills repository commits.

commands.name: audit recent
commands.description: Read recent OpsPilot audit records, including actor, action, target, risk, outcome, and warnings. Does not expose secret values.

commands.name: audit policy
commands.description: Show OpsPilot action risk boundaries: read-only, controlled mutation, high-risk plan-only, and forbidden.

commands.name: config validate
commands.description: Validate a local OpsPilot YAML config directory before committing or syncing it from GitLab.

commands.name: config status
commands.description: Show the OpsPilot backend's currently loaded config source, validity, file list, counts, warnings, and redacted credential state.

commands.name: services catalog
commands.description: Return the service catalog that maps services to repo, namespace, deployment, image, owner, middleware, config sources, and release mapping state.

commands.name: inventory overview
commands.description: Return cluster, server, workload, pod, and container overview.

commands.name: metrics health
commands.description: Check whether OpsPilot can reach configured Prometheus datasources.

commands.name: metrics datasources
commands.description: List configured Prometheus datasources, default source, and reachability.

commands.name: metrics query
commands.description: Run a read-only PromQL instant query through OpsPilot Core. Supports --source.

commands.name: metrics nodes
commands.description: Return node CPU, memory, and root filesystem usage from Prometheus. Supports --source and --source all.

commands.name: metrics pods
commands.description: Return top Pod CPU and memory usage from Prometheus, optionally filtered by namespace. Supports --source and --source all.

commands.name: metrics containers
commands.description: Return top Docker/container CPU and memory usage from cAdvisor metrics. Supports --source and --source all.

commands.name: metrics pod
commands.description: Return CPU, memory, and restart metrics for one Pod. Supports --source.

commands.name: metrics filesystems
commands.description: Return filesystem mountpoint free space, total space, and usage percent from Prometheus. Supports --source and --source all.

commands.name: k8s pods
commands.description: List Kubernetes Pods with optional namespace, status, and query filters.

commands.name: k8s logs pod
commands.description: Read Kubernetes Pod logs through pods/log, defaulting to the last 10 hours.

commands.name: docker agents
commands.description: List configured Docker node agents and reachability.

commands.name: docker containers
commands.description: List allowed Docker containers through a node agent. Supports --host and --host all.

commands.name: docker inspect
commands.description: Return read-only Docker inspect summary for one allowed container.

commands.name: docker logs
commands.description: Read short-window Docker container logs through a node agent.

commands.name: docker stats
commands.description: Return one-shot Docker container stats through a node agent.

commands.name: host disk
commands.description: Return read-only host disk evidence from a node agent: filesystem capacity, allowed path usage, Docker reclaimable bytes, container log sizes, and cleanup recommendations. Supports --host, --limit, and --depth.

commands.name: host network
commands.description: Return a short read-only host network snapshot from a node agent: interface RX/TX rates, allowed Docker container RX/TX rates, and TCP state counts. Supports --host, --limit, and --duration.

commands.name: host cleanup plan
commands.description: Return plan-only disk cleanup recommendations from host disk evidence. OpsPilot does not truncate logs, prune Docker, edit daemon config, or delete files.

commands.name: logs search
commands.description: Search collected Kubernetes logs from the configured OpenSearch/Elasticsearch backend. Supports -q, --query, and --since; requests are bounded by a short time window and result limit.

commands.name: evidence request
commands.description: Correlate APISIX access logs with service logs by host, URI, time window, optional route mapping, and evidence strength. Supports --skip-apisix / --service-only for preliminary investigation without gateway logs.

commands.name: evidence pack
commands.description: Build a unified Evidence Pack for a service, Pod, or cluster from Kubernetes, release, capability, and recent event evidence. Use --persist to store the pack server-side.

commands.name: evidence packs
commands.description: List recently persisted Evidence Packs generated by manual requests or the lightweight event-pack scanner.

commands.name: errors recent
commands.description: Return recent AI-readable error events from Kubernetes abnormal Pods, Argo CD application state, release evidence gaps, and structured file events such as middleware provisioning failures.

commands.name: context pod
commands.description: Return a Pod Evidence Pack.

commands.name: diagnose pod
commands.description: Diagnose a Pod using metrics, events, short-window logs, and release context.

commands.name: diagnose docker
commands.description: Diagnose one Docker container using inspect, stats, and short-window logs.

commands.name: inspect service
commands.description: Return a service-level inspection by service name, including release status, matching Pods, restart count, CPU, memory, Kubernetes log presence, ELK log presence, evidence gaps, and next checks.

commands.name: inspect pod
commands.description: Return a compact Pod inspection with status, restarts, events/context, metrics, Kubernetes log presence, ELK log presence, and evidence gaps.

commands.name: inspect cluster
commands.description: Return a compact cluster inspection with abnormal Pods, node resources, top Pod usage, restart evidence, and filesystem mountpoints.

commands.name: check service
commands.description: Beginner-friendly alias for inspect service. Supports --output evidence for AI-readable evidence packs.

commands.name: check pod
commands.description: Beginner-friendly alias for inspect pod. Supports --output evidence for AI-readable evidence packs.

commands.name: check cluster
commands.description: Beginner-friendly alias for inspect cluster. Supports --output evidence for AI-readable evidence packs.

commands.name: check release
commands.description: Beginner-friendly alias for release status.

commands.name: fix service
commands.description: Generate a dry-run AI fix plan for a service from OpsPilot evidence. Requires --dry-run and does not mutate repositories or clusters.

commands.name: fix pod
commands.description: Generate a dry-run AI fix plan for a Pod from OpsPilot evidence. Requires --dry-run and does not mutate repositories or clusters.

commands.name: janitor plan
commands.description: Generate a dry-run cleanup and governance plan from cluster evidence. Classifies read-only, safe, controlled, and high-risk actions; high-risk actions are plan-only.

commands.name: healer diagnose
commands.description: Diagnose a configured service release using GitLab, BuildKit, GitOps, Argo CD, Kubernetes, logs, and metrics evidence, then return safe self-healing actions and risk boundaries.

commands.name: app decommission plan
commands.description: Generate an application decommission plan for a configured service. Keeps data by default, separates GitOps workload removal from PVC/PV/hostPath/database risk, and blocks high-risk deletion from automation.

commands.name: release service
commands.description: Return a beginner-friendly release summary for one configured service, including GitLab pipeline, BuildKit, GitOps, Argo CD, latest jobs, history, gaps, and the current trigger boundary.

commands.name: release trigger
commands.description: Submit a GitLab pipeline for one configured release service through OpsPilot. This follows the standard GitLab Runner -> BuildKit -> Registry -> GitOps -> Argo CD path.

commands.name: release status
commands.description: Return a read-only release evidence chain status for one configured service.

commands.name: release evidence
commands.description: Alias for release status in the first read-only release evidence milestone.

commands.name: release diagnose
commands.description: Alias for release status with explicit evidence gaps in the first release milestone.

commands.name: release jobs
commands.description: List GitLab jobs from the latest pipeline for a configured release service.

commands.name: release logs
commands.description: Read bounded GitLab job trace logs by --job or --job-id for a configured release service.

commands.name: release history
commands.description: List GitOps release history for one configured service, including revision, image tag, current marker, author, date, and commit message.

commands.name: release rollback
commands.description: Rollback a configured service by committing a previous tag, full image, or GitOps revision to the GitOps repository. Requires --confirm and does not mutate Kubernetes directly.

commands.name: quality run service
commands.description: Submit an optional short-lived Kubernetes Job that runs the service repository .opspilot/quality.yaml API smoke and latency checks. Missing config or runner setup returns skipped/unavailable evidence and does not block release.

commands.name: quality status service
commands.description: Read the latest optional API quality Job status, logs, and JSON report for one configured release service.

commands.name: quality runner
commands.description: Internal runner used by OpsPilot-created Kubernetes Jobs. Reads OPSPILOT_QUALITY_CONFIG_JSON or --config and emits a JSON quality report.

commands.name: onboard repo
commands.description: One-command beginner onboarding for a GitLab project path. Detects the repository, generates platform Dockerfile/CI/deploy files, CPU/memory limits, probes, LimitRange, and ResourceQuota with --write, runs preflight when writing, and prints the release mapping and next steps.

commands.name: onboard service
commands.description: Generate missing Dockerfile, GitLab CI include, Kubernetes manifests, CPU/memory limits, probes, namespace guardrails, and OpsPilot release mapping from opspilot.service.yaml. Existing files are skipped unless --force is used.

commands.name: onboard check
commands.description: Check whether a service repository has Dockerfile, BuildKit GitLab CI, namespace.yaml, deploy/k8s manifests, and release mapping readiness. Supports --repo and --config.

commands.name: onboard detect
commands.description: Detect service onboarding config from a repository, including language, Dockerfile, port, build entry, tpo/devex ownership, project-level namespace mapping, and shared middleware intent.

commands.name: onboard generate
commands.description: Generate opspilot.service.yaml, GitLab CI include, Dockerfile when missing, namespace.yaml, deploy/k8s manifests, release mapping, and shared middleware intent from repository detection and project-level namespace automation.

commands.name: repo preflight
commands.description: Run automated repository governance checks for Dockerfile, platform GitLab CI include, deploy/k8s manifests, CPU/memory limits, probes, namespace guardrails, namespace ownership, port, health evidence, and shared middleware intent. Intended for CI gating.

commands.name: repo autofix
commands.description: Generate or replace platform-managed Dockerfile, .gitlab-ci.yml include, deploy/k8s manifests, opspilot.service.yaml, and release mapping. Use --write to mutate files and --force to replace existing generated surfaces.
